rulings.Case Title: MAXIMILIANO RAZO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Supplement Defendants Expert Witness Disclosure To Include Dr. Noel Weiss
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, APRIL 22, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1. Defendants Exxon Mobil Corp.¿s and Timec Company, Inc.¿s motion to supplement defendants¿ expert witness disclosure to include Dr. Noel Weiss is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
